reviews.review_text:
Kotor 2 is a complex game. 

Not because its combat is obtuse or difficult, no. If you wanna kick its ass, focus on two weapon fighting, max your strength as much as possible and look for anything that gives you bonuses to Strength, Constitution, Dex, Attack, and Defense, in that order. Spam force Speed and its upgrades and just march through the game undefeated. 

Nor because the story is somehow impenatrable. No, just pay attention to what your companions or force users say, and you'll most likely get what's going on pretty solidly by the time you reach the end. 

No the complexity comes from the fact that besides The Main Story and the expansion of character building options... Kotor 2 kinda sucks. The worlds are flat and lifeless, devoid of things to do. Theres maybe 1 or 2 quests outside the main line of each planet, and you can easily just blow right past them without any effort. The combat is even easier than it was in 1, and lemme tell you, 1 is not exactly standing toe to toe with Dark Souls, you know? Everything that isn't related to the Main Story feels shallow and poorly thought out. 

We know why this is, the game was rushed out the door and its a miracle we got a game as good as we did, but that doesn't change the fact that Kotor 2 is noticeably Less than Kotor 1 in a lot of subtle, but no less important ways. Playing through the game, you're spending what feels like 60 of the 80 hour run time in grey boxes with one or two nameless npcs walking around randomly. 

Like if you follow the usual progression people suggest, you go from Paragus Station to Citidel Station to Nar Shaddah and you only get a tiny little 1  hour break to frolic on a field on Telos before you're stuffed back in Sci-Fi Corridor #6531 for another 15 hours. You spend half the run time on Dantooine underground in a Sci-Fi Corridor, too. Korriban is a short jaunt, maybe an hour at most before you're done and out of there. So really, only Onderon offers anything different to Sci-Fi Corridor for long enough to matter. 

NPCs that aren't your companions or Force Sensitives are flat as a board, with almost zero nuance to them. Very often the names and faces are meaningless, just their role. This is The Well Meaning Friend. The Slicer. The Mandalorian. The Other Mandalorian. The Fifth Mandalorian. The Rhodian. So on and so forth. 

And the vaunted increase in depth to the game? 

It's not real. It's just bigger numbers. It doesn't create new styles of play that weren't there in Kotor 1. Kotor 2 didn't make blasters suddenly viable, in fact, Blaster Jedi was one of the strongest builds in Kotor 1.  In the end, you still have the same 5 Classes of Character. Blaster guy. Melee force user. Lightning spammer. Normal Sword Guy. And Skill Monkey. There's no reason to really delve into the complexities of the system because its not that interesting. Just stack Wisdom and pick Stasis/Fear for casters, and Stack Strength and swing two swords as a guardian. You won the game! It's not hard at all.

But why do I still have this as a reccomended game?

Because despite all that winging the story is just that good. Every world is connected to the main plot thematically. Everything revolves around a THEME. A real, genuine "I'm trying to say something substantive about Star Wars" theme. The Main Villain is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ great. They are one of the best villains in all of Star Wars because, in the end, they aren't comically evil. They have a point. A great point. Probably the best point ever had in a Star Wars story. 

And that makes them more terrifying than Palpatine or Vader ever could be. 

So yeah. I recomend the game, but I won't tell you it's the best game ever made like some people will. I won't try and hype you into buying this and playing it because that will just lead you into disappointment. No, I'll just say: "This is a game that has stuck with me for 20 years and changed how I view one of my all time favorite fictional universes, giving it more genuine philisophical depth than a thousand Legends Novels did."

Authentic Star Wars RPG!
Go dark side, go light side.
Too much fun and amazing storyline.

Its an old game and some of the restored content mods don't happen in chronological order. The influence system is also not perfect. However, a great game that tackles some serious questions about the force and is the engaging exploration if the force and a galaxy far far away ever tackled in the video game and i dare say all of the star wars medium. Don't go to nar shada as your first planet, you will get recked.

I loved playing the KOTOR games. I played the first one back on the original Xbox and have since replayed them both many times now. I would definitely recommend you pick this one up if you like Star Wars. I'd also suggest checking out some of the mods that are out as well. There are some that can enhance the game a decent bit. Overall, it's a great game.

even better than the first truly great star wars before it was ruin by disney even with how this game was rushed to completion back in the day its much better than  star wars outlaws which more than likely had more than twice the budget and time ♥♥♥♥ disney

For an unfinished game it is "OK" - maybe a 6/10. The storyline and history is incoherent and often times self-contradictory and certainly contradictory to what happened in the original game such that it makes you wonder if they even played it. Add to that for whatever reason the creators also had a hate on for the Jedi Order, yet didn't argue why or how the Jedi teachings were flawed.

Beyond that irritation though, the gameplay and missions/quests are mostly all right. Kreia is probably one of the best video game characters and so I have to give Obsidian credit on that.
Best star wars game from my childhood, just too bad they rushed the release

Knights of the old  Republic II is a technically dated mess with a fairly middling combat system that is only marginally improved over its predecessor that fortunately gets propelled to quite a satisfying experience due to the games excellent writing. 


KOTOR 2 is not perfect, the game even with the restored content mod is still unfinished, and if you are looking for an rpg or even just a Star Wars experience to get lost to and immersed in, this game probably isn't going to be for you. The environments are fairly drab and look bad even by 2005 standards, and in all honesty, the gameplay is fairly boring with barely any meaningful decisions other than what companions you fight with and the color of your lightsaber, with all of this continuing for 30+ hours. Instead KOTOR 2 should really be approached almost as if it were a piece of literature. Where its predecessor was a colorful, by the books Star Wars experience with a fairly clear good and bad, letting the player fulfill their own star wars fantasy of being a evil force lightning wielding sith, or a paragon all jedi should aspire to be, KOTOR 2 is instead about taking the role of someone disconnected from the pure duality of the force and letting yourself as a player seek to answer questions the game proposes about morality in all manner of contexts, all while managing to avoid betraying many of the themes established by the original films.

Compared to the first Knights of the old Republic, KOTOR 2 has much more mature themes, and accompanying that, much more thought out dialogue that, while not perfect, comes across as noticeably less childish than the naively good and mustache twirling evil than what came before. There is a lot more gravitas to the words that are said, and to the events that transpire which was sorely missing from KOTOR 1 as the characters feel much more real, despite the sometimes poor voice performances. Factions and characters here are much more fluid and can't be called Altruistically good, or purely and maniacally evil. This gets further expanded upon as even within your own ranks, there are a lot more interactions between characters that don't involve you which truly adds to this more broad and nuanced approach while it also helps to give the idea that your companions are not just robots waiting for you to activate. Seeing them talk about their own histories, spying on each other, or just playing a game of pazaak with one another adds so much compared to just waiting for certain triggers to advance the next companion dialogue chapters.

While ultimately a flawed, rushed game, and one that need be approached with an open mindset, KOTOR 2 certainly exceeds beyond being just a good Star Wars experience and is something that has solidified itself in a sort of special place for me.

Some notes on the technical side of things. Download the Restored Content mod (specifically the one [b]WITHOUT[/b] the m4-78 planet) from the workshop and make sure to play the game locked to 60fps and save often in multiple slots (I had 3 save slots that were rarely 10 minutes apart for context, though I never had any crashes or save corruptions.)

It pains me to write this, after raving about how much I enjoyed the first KoToR.

Is the story better than any movie or show Disney Star Wars has made in the last decade?  Sure, but that's a pitifully low bar to clear.

Did I enjoy the game enough to play from start to finish?  Yes, if nothing else so I could write this in good faith after giving the game a full chance.

Is there replay value?  Insofar as different gameplay choices lead to different outcomes and story development, sure.

So why the negative review?  

Well let's get this out of the way: if you're expecting this game to pick up where the last game left off and see what happens to your character, or any of your companions, after the events of the last game, then sorry, not happening.  You play as a completely new character with a completely new backstory; I was hoping for a Baldur's Gate 2 or Witcher 2 where you load your save data from the first game so story choices and other things carry over.  This means your choices in the first game actually matter.  With this game, seemingly nothing mattered from the first game because you're playing as the alleged last surviving Jedi.  One has to wonder if the "2" designation of this game is a slight misnomer because it implies continuity instead of a standalone story.

Yes, there may be "canonical" story outcomes and character fates in the greater Star Wars Expanded Universe mythos, but that's besides the point when playing a role playing game where you make material choices that lead to material consequences.

But even if you separate the story foundation aspect entirely from the game, there's still plenty to complain about.

Admittedly, there are some quality of life improvements from the first game like quick weapon changing, and most if not all knowledge from the first game carries over to this one.  But after that, it's hard to bring up things to praise.

There's occasional camera glitches like rotating and shifting out of view, if you have a USB controller plugged in.  Unplugging seems to resolve the issue but it's still a nuisance.  And even when there's no controller plugged in, there are some wild camera angles during some dialogue where the character speaking either isn't in view or isn't facing the camera.

Some quests are not scripted well for continuity's sake depending on certain choices that you make, which slightly breaks the roleplaying experience.  Like the Twi'lek who gambled his lady on a game of Pazaak; you can win her back over a game of Pazaak and she'll work for you as a dancer, but if you get some goons to beat up her owner she acts like she's still working off her debt to him?

There's still no rest mechanic in this game, so you either have to burn through recovery items, need equipment that has regeneration, or FP to heal.  

This game somehow managed to have even less fast travel options than the first game.  At least in the first game you could usually fast travel back to your ship but now that's no longer an option.  Most places I've seen don't have any fast travel options even after finishing all the quests there.

Looping dialog takes you out of the roleplaying experience, especially when you can go through the entire dialog tree with little to no consequence.  Some NPC's reset entirely after you finish a quest, as if you never interacted with them before.  Some dialogue choices have infinite loops.

Over thirty hours into the game, I came across a grand total of five lightsabers, all double bladed so unless a Jedi character has the two weapon feat they are at a significant disadvantage if not outright useless.

In the first game, every companion characters had their own unique quest line which expanded their story and relations with the player character; in this game, companion characters have an influence system which seems to require them to be in your party in order for you to make choices that either increase or decrease influence with them, and only once you reach a certain threshold do more quest lines become available.  

Some recorded dialogue is hilariously inaccurate with the subtitles.

And finally, the ending is completely anti-climactic and very few plot threads are resolved, including that of your companions.  In fact, most of your companions don't appear at all in the endgame and only are mentioned through optional dialog in the very end.  Come to find out, this game is notorious for having cut content, including cut endings.  Yes, mods exist that attempt to restore cut content, but this review is based solely on what's available out of the box for this game.

So really, even though there are some positives with this game, and even though I'm glad my curiosity after the first KoToR is now settled, I can say if I knew everything above, I don't think I'd have bothered playing this game.  If you really enjoyed the first KoToR, save your time and energy and leave the fate of your character and companions to your imagination, or just read any Old Republic Star Wars comics and books published prior to the Disney acquisition.
This game helped make me into a cynical overly philosophical pessimist for years join us
A great star wars game. It might be my favorite star wars game ever made. Still even 2026 worth a playthrough or even more.

Ļoti forša Zvaigžņu Karu spēle. Daudz izvēles, daudz ceļošanas starp planētām ar savu kuģi.

Var arī just elementus kuri iedvesmoja "Mass Effect" triloģiju.

Still even on my 6th+ playthrough with RCM, one of my favorite games of all time, and an absolute triumph of character and dialogue writing. Deserves a faithful yet modernized remaster more than any other game I can think of.
its kotor 2. both kotor games are great play options. might have to mod out some bugs but well worth it.

They took the building blocks Kotor created and expanded upon them further than ever.

The combat and world are virtually the same style as the first, but the story and characters are expanded so much further. A great amount of depth and morality comes into play with this story, as well as some of the best villains Star Wars has created.

Also this is infinitely more stable than the first game.
incredible game probably my favorite star wars game of all time, rcm basically required though

It's an Obsidian RPG. It plays well, and has a decent story set in the Star Wars universe.

Endgame felt a little forced however.
Don't pass up the KOTOR series. played this one with the restored content mod. had a great time. I'll add KOTOR 2 as one of my favorite games of all time.

Just started and kind of enjoying it so far. One thing that is bugging me is the prologue is terrible mixed with terrible writing.  Never felt so lost in whose my character supposed to be. And the dialogue options are like you know exactly what's going on, who everyone is etc. NPC keep talking to like I'm supposed to know everything. Missing way to much context for the way this prologue is written. IT would be better if this was my 2nd play through of this game cause that's the only way you'd know anything or say any of the other dialogue. Beat the first game and it tells me nothing for what's going on in this prologue. Kills the vibe and immersion for me.

First game def is better in my opinion at the moment.

KOTOR 2 Is one of my favourite Western RPGs of all time, and it's a masterpiece that defines what Obsidian does best. With that being said, the game is completely unplayable on PC, EVEN with patches, EVEN with workshop addons, EVEN with mods, the game just doesn't prop run propely, and it's full of bugs that will 100% sure disrupt either your installation process or the gameplay itself. Your best chance to experience this game is getting an OG Xbox.

Im a big rpg game type of guy and ive played everything from skyrim to fallout NV and the witcher series but not one game hit this good like KOTOR 2. from build crafting to planets dialogue options i was never so invested into a story of an rpg like this before it starts slow and boring yes but please give the game a chance trust me you wont regret it

finally gave it a try after being glitched out on original xbox version back when it came out. With the TSLRCM mod to fix things, I think this is my favorite star wars story and one of the best rpgs ive ever played

Its rather clunky and difficult to move around - unlike other PC based RPGs where you use your mouse/trac ball and the WSAD system. I had a difficult time getting into the game because movement of my character was problematic and jerky.

I have a great time with this every single playthrough, excellent build-crafting, amazing characters, and a strong story. Worth the time if you enjoy Star Wars as a franchise or if you just like robust RPGs. Install the Restored Content Mod too if you like, although admittedly it can add unnecessary confusion due to several strange occurrences. Either way, great experience overall.

Great game and well worth the price, especially if you get it on sale.

However, this is a very buggy port and it can take a lot of enjoyment away from it.

For example, I was at the last part of the game of the game and all of the dialogue and cutscenes were bugged and skipped, it was very annoying and a disappointing way to end the game.

However, I believe there are mods to fix this but still be warned. 

Easy 9/10 game  - 6/10 pc port

The superior of the 2 Kotor games.  The story is leaps and bounds better than Kotor 1 and rivals many other RPGs of the era, maybe of all time.  The mechanics aren't for everybody (turn-based but somehow still live-action), but RPG fans will love it.  Only negative is the visuals are EXTREMELY dated.  Like, worse than Kotor 1.  But if you can look past that and enjoy it for what it is, you'll have 40-50 hours of enjoyment.  NOTE: Installing the cut-content mod on steam is easy and a MUST for the full experience!

Played this on the OG XBOX and its one of the best story games ive ever played. Gameplay is garbage compared to nowadays but its easy to look past for the story of the game and your companions. (PSA play with restored content mod. Such an improved experience)

It's a classic.
Basically, it's like the first one, but with improved graphics and gameplay, and the bugs and flaws from the first game fixed.
Even though it's one of the first games I remember that lets you choose different endings based on your actions, the game is very linear, just like games used to be back then. But it's worth it—a little nostalgia every now and then.
-----------------------
Es un clásico.
Básicamente es como el primero pero añadiendo gráficos, jugabilidad, y corrigiendo los bugs y defectos del 1.
A pesar de ser uno de los primeros que recuerde, que dan la opción de establecer distintos finales según tus actos, el juego es muy lineal como solían ser en aquella época. Pero vale la pena, un poco de nostalgia de vez en cuando.

Statement: Quite possibly one of the best games of all time. No doubt a top Star Wars game of all time. It's so easy to dive into and get absolutely lost in. Great characters, great story, great critique of Star Wars.

Unnecessary addition: Downloading the restored content mod is optional but highly encouraged. There is a great deal of enjoyment to be had with it. On top of that, it adds plenty more meatbags to be killed.
Its KOTOR 2... the greatest damn RPG based on Star Wars..  ever.  Easily top 5 video games of all time!
Not finished yet but pretty cool game. Highly recommend to SW legends fans.

Played the normal Steam PC version, had to use 1280x720 to have the movie cut scenes show in game.  (1600x900 would not play some of the important longer cut scenes).   As the games character models are so low poly it looks almost the same at 1280x720 as 1600x900, using 4xAA.
Overall a great game, one of the best stories ever in a video game, gameplay is a bit clunky managing 3 members in fights usually, and the way they make the game hard in spots is by swarming you with enemies.   
Some episodes are a bit boring especially the 3rd level (Citadel Station).
Not as good as KOTOR1 which is an all round better game 9/10
but KOTOR2 is still a solid 7.5/10
great game, runs on the most potato of pcs pretty well even with TSLRCM

Game holds true to time. I'm enjoying this game more than any AAA title. Kinda says a lot. Great story, so many details that make it fluid, engaging, difficult, balanced. The puzzles are fun. Combat and progression guidance are on point.

No complaints at all, worth every dollar. Super engaging. You get sucked in to the story.

Better than the first one in almost every way possible. 
Pretty buggy though, and it overall feels unfinished at times.

Such an amazing game, would recommend installing TSLRCM through deadly stream instead of steam workshop so you don't have to play through the M4-78 Enhancement Project because it really ruined the flow of the game for me on my first playthrough.

4/5 
I was worried my Gen Z brain wouldn't be able to get past the first few planets but now that I beat the game I'm happy I did. The gameplay is outdated for sure and minor bugs led to some frustrations BUT
this game is still peak.
The story and dialogue has stood the test of time along with the companions and their questlines. I appreciate that this game took risks in certain sections and if you do intend to play and complete the game I recommend the Restored Content Mod, as it helps add in some companion centric story moments that were left cut for time for the game's OG release.
Both Dark and Light side playthroughs are fun and dark side powers aren't exclusive to Sith playthroughs (and vice versa for Light Side powers).

Best part of my childhood I came back 20 years later to play this game and it's still one of the best star wars games I have ever played With the re-textures and the restored content mods this game is still worth playing 20 + years later 

Also being a 20 year old game it can run on a potato or a smart phone so there is a 99% chance your PC can run this game I'm playing it on a Lenovo Think Pad Business Laptop when traveling for work LOL 

If you have not played KOTOR 1 yet please go buy that first and play it or this game will not make sense

The best Star Wars role-playing game, takes place during an era of Star Wars that has not been beaten to death by movies, made by a developer that at one time was known for some of gaming's best rpgs and, although the graphics are slightly out of date it should be a must play title for anybody interested in Star Wars or good roleplaying games.

Its a classic. I remember buying this game the day it came out on the OG xbox and playing days upon days. It offered a challenge and was unlike anything I had ever played at the time. I still holds up today. Some minor settings adjustments must be made to play on modern machines but other than that its exactly how I remember it! One of the GOATS of star wars games.
I've played this game since I was a kid and it never gets old. And mods bring a whole new light to this already wonderful story.
10/10 game, but what am I a Sith Lord with a nanny? Kreia will not stop complaining about me murdering people. My bad, Mom, I just need the Dark Side points.

I knew I fell in love with  game a when I played it for Seven hours overnight at a friend's house. I'm not the biggest Star Wars fan but I really do like this game. I'm sorry it was never truly finished but playing this did get me to play the original. I played this several times since and I will play it many times more.

This game, like Kreia, fills me with conflicting emotions and makes me gaslight myself at points.
Do not install the Restored Content M4-78 version on the workshop.  If you get a game-breaking bug on Nar Shaddaa with the transponder, you'll love this review.

When it's good, it's so good it stands tall above the entire franchise, easily mogging even the Episode 3 PS2 game and the JEDI series by EA.  When it's bad, woah buddy, it SUCKS. I get that Obsidian, yet again, ran out of time and had to rush yet another game like they did with NV, but around the midpoint this game falls apart and miraculously manages to piece itself back together before the end. Unlike New Vegas though, mods don't really elevate this game, and in the case of M4-78, detriments it.

The writing is excellent. Kreia is probably the most layered character in the entire franchise to date. Every choice is, like their later game New Vegas, so perfectly morally grey and nuanced that you don't feel trapped in the arbitrary video-gamey limits of "Good choice vs comically evil choice". Kreia wants you to use the dark side as a tool, but not succumb to how easy it makes it, and the game reflects that well. I loved the story, even with the ending being sequel-bait for 1) a game that never happened, and 2) something now rendered non-canon by Kathleen Kennedy.  I did feel like my choices mattered though, which very few RPGs actually accomplish, but one thing stood out to me: you get teased about being able to redeem yourself toward the end if you did Dark Side path (which I did because Force Crush slaps), but once you hit the Dark Side cap, you can't gain Light Side points.  Feels half-baked and unfinished, like a lot of other parts of the game.

The companions are mostly better than KotoR 1. Atton is pretty under-developed once you find out his origins, Mira doesn't really progress, and Bao-Dur seems unfinished.  But Visas (my favourite), Kreia, Canderous/Mandalore, HK47 and T3 are all great. The Disciple is a bit of a mixed bag since he wants you to adhere to videogame-y moral goodness, but when you do appease that, he's solid.  I didn't care for GO-TO at all, also seemed unfinished.

The villains are trash, though. Sion had a cool backstory but was a bipolar simp for the most part, and Nihilus doesn't even speak despite being this world-devouring Force demigod, and dies like a chump against you. Malak in KotoR 1 had presence, all through the game you would hear of his schemes and machinations and it built beautifully up to that final confrontation on the Star Forge.  In its enriched writing of Kreia, all the other antagonists suffered.

The visuals have aged really well for 2004, and probably has my favourite art direction of the franchise.

Compatibility wise, this game has caused me some headaches. I played KOTOR 1 so I knew the play: get rivatuner, cap the game at 60fps with it.  I thought that would be enough, since this is supposedly the version of KotoR that got the most love especially with its Steam revamp in the 2010s, but I've had so many weird oddities. I've crashed twice.  Cutscenes sometimes caused my whole PC to lag and then Windows Explorer would die.  Returning to Dantooine near the end of the game turned my entire monitor off for 20 seconds.  I fell through the floor on Malachor V.  After 1-2 hours of play, dialogue would rapidly skip during cutscenes and I would have to replay a whole section to get the context I missed.  And now the worst one: I had a game breaking bug on Nar Shaddaa during the Transponder Card portion of the quest: I would select my two companions to go onto Goto's ship, and then the game would play the cutscene, but after it, I would still be on Nar Shaddaa with my previous party.   Thankfully, someone had this same bug and I found the only solution was to go into the game's .ini config file and add a line to enable console commands, then use the command "warp 351nar" , lo-and-behold, I was teleported onto the ship and was able to beat the game normally thereafter.   This bug was particularly frustrating because it happened after one of the worst sequences in gaming history, where Atton, Mira and T3 had to escape hordes of enemies and if like me, you hyper-focused on your Force-Sensitive party members like Visas and Kreia, your run of the mill blaster users absolutely sucked and this section was brutal.  No Borderless Windowed option is an L, and given the game's revamp in the 2010s, they could have added that.  HK-47 duplicated himself somehow and I played the whole game having two of him on my ship.

I also want to recommend you do not install the restored content mod on the Workshop that includes "M4-78".  This part of the game, unbeknownst to me was added by the mod, which EVERYONE recommended I install for years.   It is single-handedly one of the worst, most boring parts of any game I have ever played.  So much walking. So much backtracking.  Your own character can even tell the droids how tedious it is, so I can see why it was cut and if you don't want the most boring 3 hours of your life wasted, please do not install it.

The combat is my favourite part of this game.  Yes, it's turn-based, but unlike KotoR 1 having a really up-and-down progression, KotoR 2 allows you to spec into your desired build early and the payoffs are huge.   I love that Visas mirrors what you learn and she becomes your apprentice of sorts, copying you during combat. I love Force Storm and Force Crush-ing my way through dozens of enemies who barely leave a scratch on me even on the harder difficulty.  Destroy Droid on hordes of HK units make you feel like more of a god than the mid Force Unleashed games went out of their way to instil in you with awful Starkiller. Playing a Dark Side user has never felt this good. 

The problem, however, is structure.  This game swaps POVs more than a George R.R. Martin novel.  I thought it was going to be isolated to the prologue with T3, but nope, you will be swapping characters well over 10 times throughout the playthrough and not all characters were made equal. The above Nar Shaddaa part was bloody miserable and I'd rather hack my genitals off and become a Hollow Knight fan than ever play it again.  You play as droids multiple times, you play as Mira, useless Atton, but you never get to be Visas outside of one part of the game, and you can never be Kreia.  I would much rather the player character play the bulk of the sections: very few games pull off constant perspective switching, MGS2 and Cyberpunk 2077 are the only good examples off the top of my head.

Overall I would rate this game a 6/10. 60% of it is some of the best there ever was, but that 40% makes me want to deepthroat a Mossberg shotgun and paint my ceiling.

Playing this on Steam is an exercise in futility, when on a NEW apple computer.

X-box controllers do NOT function on KotOR2, only keyboard and mouse .. which is typical for 99% of all macOs games on steam .. limited in resources and chocked performance/fps.

I wouldnt waste another cent on anything in Steam & stick to consoles .. where you control your gaming experience; not 3rd party gatekeepers.

**note** .. KotOR 2 is literally the best video game EVER made .. EVER.  Simply put, Steam is NOT the place for this game, or most others.
Oldie but Goldie... I played this game decades ago and it still is good.
I wish there were way more workshop mods for this but its a good game once you get past the 5 second quicksave and load after every battle (stuck after enemy killed)
This game sucks. Even with the mandatory mod patches to fix constant crashes.

The KOTOR series is a timless one. Sadly this iteration is horribly broken, despite trying countell online FPS limiting "fixes" the character will not move at all after combat -none working- requiring a full save and reload each time. I tried for 90 minutes before I decided my time and sanity is worth more to me. Shameful port. Do better.
Just too dated for I, a life-long Star Wars fan who loves the sequel SWTOR, to enjoy.
Kotor II, buggy mess that it is, remains one of my favorite time sinks.
Classic game. Must play for Star Wars fans.
proof that obsidian always works the best under tight deadlines
Great game overall. love the mods that you can enable

So you sirs, you ALL are DISNEY ADULTS! As I am! <3 Star Wats, Marvel, Princesses, Miley Cyrus, and everyone's favorite, Jimmy KIMMEL himself!
classic game that even now is still a blast to play
What a masterpiece. They really don't make em' like they used to.

If you love Star Wars, this game is perfect for you.
great game ,better than the last 3 movies!!

I liked it, that should be enough
